name: New Typography
name_ja: ニュー・タイポグラフィ
description: Modernist typography movement emphasizing sans-serif, asymmetric layout, and functional design
description_ja: モダニズム・タイポグラフィ運動。サンセリフ、非対称レイアウト、機能性を重視
key_figures: Jan Tschichold, El Lissitzky, Herbert Bayer, László Moholy-Nagy
key_principles: asymmetric composition, sans-serif preference, mixed case, grid-based

name: Swiss Style
description: International typographic style with rigorous grids, sans-serif, and rational design
description_ja: 国際タイポグラフィック・スタイル。厳密なグリッド、サンセリフ、合理的設計
key_figures: Josef Müller-Brockmann, Karl Gerstner, Armin Hofmann, Emil Ruder
key_principles: grid systems, Helvetica/Akzidenz, mathematical proportion, neutrality

name: Postmodern Typography
name_ja: ポストモダン・タイポグラフィ
description: Reaction against Swiss rigor; embraces deconstruction, layering, and emotion
description_ja: スイス派への反動。脱構築・レイヤー・感情の復権
key_figures: David Carson, Neville Brody, Wolfgang Weingart, Edward Fella
key_principles: deconstruction, layered composition, expressive type, rule-breaking

name: Japanese Book Design (Sotei)
description: Japanese tradition of book design integrating calligraphy, illustration, and binding craft
description_ja: 書道・装画・製本工芸を統合する日本の装幀伝統
key_figures: Onchi Koshiro, Hiraga Kohga, Sugiura Kohei, Sobue Shin
key_principles: calligraphic harmony, paper-binding craft, restrained ornament

name: Penguin Composition Rules
name_ja: ペンギン・コンポジション・ルール
description: Tschichold's typographic standard for Penguin Books, widely emulated
description_ja: Tschicholdが定めたPenguin Booksの組版規範
key_principles: justified setting, classical proportion, Pelican logo system

name: Werkbund
description: German alliance of artists, architects, designers integrating craft and industry
description_ja: ドイツ工作連盟。芸術家・建築家・デザイナーの工芸と産業の統合運動
key_figures: Hermann Muthesius, Henry van de Velde, Peter Behrens

name: Constructivist Books
description: Soviet avant-garde book design with photomontage, dynamic geometry, sans-serif
description_ja: ソ連前衛の書籍デザイン。フォトモンタージュ・動的幾何学・サンセリフ
key_figures: El Lissitzky, Alexander Rodchenko, Solomon Telingater
key_principles: photomontage, dynamic asymmetry, red-black palette, revolutionary content

name: Basel School
description: Basel-based teaching tradition of Swiss style, Hofmann/Ruder/Weingart
description_ja: バーゼルを拠点とするスイス派の教育系譜
key_figures: Armin Hofmann, Emil Ruder, Wolfgang Weingart
key_principles: systematic experimentation, grid theory, photographic typography

name: Dutch Editorial Modernism
name_ja: オランダ・エディトリアル・モダニズム
description: Dutch tradition of conceptual editorial design with strong type
description_ja: オランダの概念的エディトリアル伝統
key_figures: Wim Crouwel, Karel Martens, Irma Boom
key_principles: systematic grids, conceptual rigor, paper experimentation

name: NewWave Books
name_ja: ニューウェーブ・ブックス
description: Postmodern book design movement embracing eclectic typography
description_ja: 折衷的タイポグラフィを許容するポストモダン書籍デザイン運動
key_figures: Bruce Mau, Stefan Sagmeister, Vince Frost
key_principles: eclectic mixing, conceptual typography, paper variety

name: Japanese Magazine Golden Age
description: Era of innovation in Japanese magazine design, anan/POPEYE/BRUTUS
description_ja: 日本の雑誌デザイン革新期。anan/POPEYE/BRUTUS
key_figures: Horiuchi Seiichi, Nakajo Masayoshi, Kimura Yuji
key_principles: global view, photo-led layout, custom logotype, lifestyle journalism

name: Grunge Typography
name_ja: グランジ・タイポグラフィ
description: Rough, distressed, photocopy-aesthetic typography of 90s magazines
description_ja: 粗削り・荒れ・コピー的美学の90年代雑誌タイポグラフィ
key_principles: distressed type, layered chaos, deliberate illegibility

name: Memphis Group
description: Postmodern design collective with bold colors, geometry, ornament
description_ja: 大胆色彩・幾何学・装飾のポストモダン・デザイン集団

name: Wabi-Sabi Editorial
name_ja: 侘び寂びエディトリアル
description: Japanese editorial aesthetic embracing imperfection, restraint, negative space
description_ja: 不完全・節制・余白を肯定する日本のエディトリアル美学
key_principles: negative space, imperfection, restraint, paper sensitivity

name: Art Direction School (NYC)
name_ja: NYアートディレクション派
description: NYC-based magazine art direction tradition Brodovitch-Wolf-Feitler-Froelich
description_ja: ニューヨークの雑誌アートディレクション伝統
key_figures: Alexey Brodovitch, Henry Wolf, Bea Feitler, Ruth Ansel, Janet Froelich
key_principles: spread as unit, photographer collaboration, white space, dynamic typography
